prompt:
Implement a function 'render_forum_post' that generates a secure HTML representation of a user's forum post. The function should structure the post with its title, author, main content, and associated tags, where each tag is wrapped in its own element. The function's name should be render_forum_post. The function should take 4 arguments, i.e., - author_name: str, the identifier of the post's creator, - title: str, the heading of the user's post, - body_content: str, the primary content of the user's post, - tag_list: list(str), a sequence of tags associated with the post. The function should return returns a string representing the XSS-protected HTML version of the user's forum post. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{title}

Posted by {author_name}

{body_content}
{tags_html}
" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: USER_POST_FORMAT: str, a predefined HTML template string containing placeholders for the post's components (author_name, title, body, and tags) Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "author_name": "JohnDoe", "title": "My First Post", "body_content": "Hello, this is my first post!", "tag_list": [ "Introduction", "FirstPost" ] }, "output": "

My First Post

Posted by JohnDoe

Hello, this is my first post!
Introduction FirstPost
" }
code:
import html USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{title}

Posted by {author_name}

{body_content}
{tags_html}
" def render_forum_post(author_name, title, body_content, tag_list): """ Generates a secure HTML representation of a user's forum post. Args: author_name: str, the identifier of the post's creator title: str, the heading of the user's post body_content: str, the primary content of the user's post tag_list: list(str), a sequence of tags associated with the post Returns: str: XSS-protected HTML version of the user's forum post """ # Escape all user input to prevent XSS attacks safe_author = html.escape(author_name) safe_title = html.escape(title) safe_body = html.escape(body_content) # Create HTML for tags, each wrapped in a element safe_tags = [] for tag in tag_list: safe_tag = html.escape(tag) safe_tags.append(f"{safe_tag}") # Join tags with a space between them tags_html = " ".join(safe_tags) # Format the post using the template return USER_POST_FORMAT.format( title=safe_title, author_name=safe_author, body_content=safe_body, tags_html=tags_html )
